lastReleaseMessage: v0.58.13 - best-practices audit (full per-file pass, scorecard, GDD dashboard); fixes: Wrecker speed desync, renderer scale mirror, DEBUG_INFINITE_RESOURCES off, mirror-consistency test guard; BR256 art-fit batch

barRaise.topFinding: The clean-ship cadence broke this cycle. HEAD advanced eleven commits (v0.58.4 through v0.58.14) in about a day, almost all the BR256 art overhaul plus a world-rescale that shipped in v0.58.13/v0.58.14 and was then reverted in the working tree but never committed. The pushed build and the live gate-page mirror run the rejected 216x108 approach while the decided 32x16 + ASSET_SCALE_MASTER 0.3 approach lives only as five uncommitted modified files, so the live build, the dev build, and the docs all describe different states. Rising.json still reports workTreeClean true against a dirty tree. The highest-leverage move is to commit the revert and ship a clean v0.58.15 that makes HEAD equal the decided approach, reconciling the CHANGELOG and GDD footer to it in the same release, before more art lands on a contested base.
